_about: ACTIVE query-expansion rules consumed by search_v2.decompose_query at decompose time. These bridge a genuine vocabulary gap between how a diner phrases a query and how venues describe themselves, when the embedding abstraction alone misses. Each rule appends `add` terms to a decomposed `field` when any `trigger` substring appears in the raw query. Eval-gate before adding (see learn.py).

rules.note: Bare 'meat-forward' embeds toward burger joints; BBQ/grill/steakhouse/chophouse venues (Smokestak, Temper, Blacklock) rank 200-1100 in the tags index and miss the top-100 retrieval cut. Adding meat sub-type vocabulary pulls them into the pool (7/8 into top-100, Smokestak rank 7). Verified 2026-06-17.
